steps.title: Rewrite as a quadratic and set up the discriminant
steps.content:
Shortcut (discriminant): Expand $-(2x-5)^2 = p + 11$ into standard form:
$-(4x^2 - 20x + 25) = p + 11$
$4x^2 - 20x + (36 + p) = 0$
For no real solution, the discriminant must be negative: $b^2 - 4ac \lt 0$.

steps.title: Solve the discriminant inequality for p
steps.content:
$(-20)^2 - 4(4)(36+p) \lt 0$
$400 - 576 - 16p \lt 0$
$-176 - 16p \lt 0$
$p \gt -11$
The least integer greater than $-11$ is $p = -10$. That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
